auth.twoFactorExpiry: The code is valid for 10 minutes.
auth.tooManyAttempts: Too many requests. Please wait a moment.
auth.emailVerificationSent: A confirmation link was sent to your email address.
auth.resetSent: If an account with this email exists, a reset link has been sent.
auth.resetSuccess: Password changed successfully. You can now log in.
auth.releaseModeShamirEmailDesc: Code sent by email to contacts, decryption in viewer
auth.releaseModeShamirPrintDesc: Code handed over physically, decryption in viewer
auth.emailVerified: Email verified successfully. You can now log in.
auth.accountDeleted: Your account has been deleted successfully.
auth.tokenExpiredMessage: The confirmation link has expired. Please request a new one in the settings.
auth.loginSubtitle: Log in and confirm access with the 2FA code.
auth.resetVaultDeletionWarning: Resetting your password permanently deletes all existing vault entries. This is required by zero-knowledge: without your old password, nobody can decrypt them.
auth.verifyPendingDescription: We sent a confirmation link to {email}. Click the link in the email — then you can sign in.
auth.verifyPendingHint: Didn't receive anything? Check your spam folder, or request a new email below.
auth.resendSent: If the address is registered with us, a new confirmation email has been sent.
auth.emailNotVerifiedLogin: Please confirm your email address before signing in.

hero.title: So your family knows what they need to know.
hero.subtitle: When you can't tell them anymore — illness, accident, dementia or death.

vault.title: Encrypted Vault
vault.desc: Store passwords, bank details, documents and personal messages. Everything is encrypted directly in your browser — before it reaches the server.

contacts.title: Emergency Contacts
contacts.desc: You decide who gets access in an emergency and how many people need to cooperate.

zeroKnowledge.title: Only you hold the key
zeroKnowledge.desc: Your data is encrypted so that neither Heritavo nor anyone else can read it — only you and the people you trust.

autoRelease.title: Automatic Emergency Plan
autoRelease.desc: You check in regularly — one click is enough. If the check-in is missed, Heritavo automatically starts your emergency plan.

methods.title: 3 Release Methods
methods.desc: From a printed Recovery Sheet to split access codes for multiple people — you choose what fits your situation.

offline.title: Works Offline
offline.desc: Your contacts don't need an Heritavo account. With offline methods, a browser is enough — no internet required.

q1.a: Heritavo is a Swiss service that we host and operate for you.

q2.a: Only you — and in an emergency, the people you trust. Your data is encrypted so we technically cannot read it.

q3.a: It depends on the method. With Recovery Sheet, no one knows. With Shamir, contacts receive their code during setup.

q4.q: How does the emergency plan work?
q4.a: After the set period without a sign of life, your contacts receive an email with a link to the encrypted vault.

q5.a: 3 methods: Recovery Sheet (offline), access code by email, printed access code. In every case, the released data package can be decrypted locally in the browser — no internet connection needed.

q6.q: What if an access code is lost?
q6.a: With a Recovery Sheet, you can re-download it in Settings (master password required). Warning: if the Recovery Sheet was stolen, it can be used to decrypt the vault — in that case, rotate the release key in Settings and print a new sheet. With Shamir codes, you can redo the setup in the dashboard and distribute new codes. Your vault data is preserved in all cases.

q8.q: Are limits shared in a household?
q8.a: Yes — entries, attachments and storage are shared across the household. Only emergency contacts are per user. Example: In the Family plan, all 5 users share the 400 entries and 300 attachments.

s1.title: Create account & choose method
s1.desc: Sign up and choose how your emergency plan should work.

s2.title: Set up emergency plan
s2.desc: Add your trusted contacts and make sure everyone has received their access code.

s3.title: Fill vault & check in
s3.desc: Store your data securely. Then a regular click serves as your sign of life.

pro.features: 1 User|200 Entries|3 Emergency Contacts|100 Attachments (200 MB each)|3 legacy videos (500 MB each)

couple.features: 2 Users|200 Entries|5 Emergency Contacts|150 Attachments (200 MB each)|5 legacy videos (500 MB each)

family.desc: For families up to five people.
family.features: 5 Users|400 Entries|8 Emergency Contacts|300 Attachments (200 MB each)|10 legacy videos (500 MB each)

landing.heroBadge: Heritavo · End-to-end encrypted · Made in Switzerland
landing.heroSubtitle: Illness, accident, dementia or death — Heritavo makes sure your loved ones have access to what matters when you can't tell them yourself. You decide who learns what, and when.
landing.heroImageAlt: Grandmother and granddaughter smiling while looking at a smartphone together

flowDemo.title: And when it happens?
flowDemo.subtitle: This is how the emergency plan unfolds — fully automatic, step by step.

s1.title: Sign of life
s1.desc: One click is enough — everything stays locked.

s4.title: Family gets access
s4.desc: Your contacts receive the encrypted vault.

zkDemo.sample: The spare key is in the safe deposit box
zkDemo.caption: Encrypted live with AES-256-GCM in your browser — nothing is sent anywhere. The key stays with you: we couldn't read your data even if we wanted to.

landing.featuresSubtitle: Heritavo doesn't just store data — it makes sure your family knows what to do, whenever the moment comes.
landing.stepsTitle: Digital legacy planning in three steps
landing.stepsSubtitle: Set up once — everything runs automatically after that.

modes.label: Release Modes
modes.title: 3 ways to secure your vault
modes.subtitle: With every method, the key stays exclusively with you and your trusted contacts — never with us.

recovery.title: Recovery Sheet
recovery.desc: You print a Recovery Key and store it securely — in a safe, with a notary or in a bank vault. In an emergency, it is entered in the included viewer to open the vault.
recovery.pros: Completely without internet|No trusted contacts needed|Easy to set up
recovery.cons: Whoever finds the sheet has access|Physical security is crucial

email.title: Access code by email
email.desc: Each trusted contact receives a personal code by email during setup. In an emergency, everyone enters their code in the included viewer — the fragment is decrypted locally. Heritavo never sees the code.
email.pros: Convenient for trusted contacts|No printing needed|Multiple people required for access
email.cons: Codes must be stored securely|Email access required during setup

print.title: Printed access code
print.desc: You print a code for each trusted contact and hand it over in person. In an emergency, everyone enters their code in the included viewer — no internet needed.
print.pros: No digital transmission|Personal handover|Completely offline capable
print.cons: Physical handover required|Codes must be stored securely

modes.noticeTitle: The key is with you — not with us
modes.noticeDesc1: For all 3 methods: Heritavo cannot recover a lost access code. This is by design — because what we cannot see, we cannot lose or share.
modes.noticeDesc2: If an access code is lost, you can regenerate it in Settings or the Dashboard. If a code was stolen, we recommend regenerating all codes — old ones become automatically invalid. Your vault data is always preserved.

security.label: Technology
security.title: Security is our foundation
security.desc: Heritavo encrypts exclusively in your browser. Your private data never leaves your device in plain text.
security.items: AES-256-GCM Encryption (Web Crypto API)|Argon2id Key Derivation — no brute force|Your data never leaves the device in plain text|Hosted in Switzerland (heritavo.com)|Open-source decryption tool for emergencies
security.codeComment1: // Everything happens locally in your browser

landing.pricingSubtitle: Choose the protection that fits your life. Cancel anytime.
landing.pricingNote: All plans are billed as a one-time payment per term · Cancel anytime (effective next cycle)

cta.desc: Start free — no credit card. In a few minutes your family will know what they need to know.

about.label: About Heritavo
about.title: Swiss zero-knowledge vault for digital legacy
about.body: Heritavo grew out of a simple observation: when someone dies or becomes incapacitated, important digital assets systematically get lost today — crypto holdings, insurance policies, recovery codes, personal messages. Classical password managers only address this as an afterthought. Heritavo is built from the ground up for exactly this inheritance use case: browser-side encryption (AES-256-GCM, Argon2id), automated emergency plan via check-in, three sharing modes including Shamir's Secret Sharing — and full sovereignty, because Heritavo mathematically never sees your data.
about.founderLine: Behind Heritavo is Moritz Hauff from Tägerwilen, Thurgau — software engineer and founder. Hosted in Switzerland, Swiss Data Protection Act (revDSG).

landing.footerDesc: So your family knows what they need to know. Developed and hosted in Switzerland.

moments.label: Three moments when life suddenly turns
moments.title: Planning isn't only for later.
moments.subtitle: Heritavo makes your family capable of acting — before they have to.

sudden.title: Suddenly ill
sudden.desc: Stroke, serious accident, coma. With Heritavo, your loved ones know where to find insurance details, power of attorney and banking access — ready for the moment when you can't act yourself.

cognitive.title: Slowly forgetting
cognitive.desc: Dementia, Alzheimer's. You no longer remember passwords or contracts — your partner has to take over, without being left in the dark.

death.title: Suddenly gone
death.desc: An accident, a heart attack. Your loved ones have to cancel accounts, end contracts and find your personal messages — they can't call you anymore.

cookie.text: This website uses only technically necessary cookies (session) and cookies from the payment provider Stripe. For anonymous reach measurement we use self-hosted Umami Analytics — no cookies, no personal data, hosted in Switzerland.

dashboard.securityStatusDesc: Your presence is the prerequisite for the confidentiality of your data.
dashboard.zeroKnowledgeDesc: Your data is encrypted exclusively in your browser before it reaches our server.
dashboard.zeroKnowledgeInfo: Even our administrators can never read your data in plain text. Your master password is the only key.
dashboard.setupWarningShamir: The vault can only be filled once all contacts have been provided with their access code.
dashboard.setupWarningContact: Please add at least one emergency contact before filling the vault. Without a contact, nobody can be notified.
dashboard.releaseActiveDesc: Your vault has been released to your emergency contacts.
dashboard.releaseActiveHint: If this was an error (e.g. you were on vacation and unreachable), you can cancel the release here.
dashboard.cancelReleaseConfirm: Really cancel the emergency release? Your data will no longer be shared and your check-in timer will restart.
dashboard.releaseCancelled: Release cancelled — please check in now to reset the timer.
dashboard.setupIncompleteDesc: You have no emergency contacts set. In an emergency, no one can access your vault – the automatic release would have no recipient.

vault.title: Vault
vault.attachmentTooltip: Allowed file types: PDF, Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Text, CSV, JSON, Images (JPG, PNG, GIF, WebP, SVG), ZIP, Certificates/Keys (PEM, CRT, P12, PUB, PPK, id_rsa, …)
vault.noEntries: No entries in the vault yet.
vault.setupIncomplete: Emergency setup incomplete. Please distribute all access codes to contacts first.
vault.setupContactNeeded: Please add at least one emergency contact first.
vault.unlockFailed: Unlock failed. Please check your password.
vault.enterMasterPassword: Enter your master password to continue.
vault.setupIncompleteShamir: Emergency setup incomplete – new entries can only be added after completion
vault.setupIncompleteNoContact: No emergency contact – existing entries cannot be delivered in an emergency
vault.setupIncompleteShamirDesc: Complete the setup (distribute codes/fragments to contacts). Existing entries remain fully intact and do not need to be re-saved.
vault.setupIncompleteNoContactDesc: Add at least one emergency contact so your data is accessible in an emergency.
vault.unsupportedHint: Allowed: PDF, Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Text, CSV, JSON, Images (JPG/PNG/GIF/WebP/SVG), ZIP, Certificates (PEM/CRT/P12).
vault.uploadPartial: An attachment upload failed. Entry partially saved.

export.title: Export Data
export.unlockDesc: To create an export of your data, you must first unlock your vault.
export.readyDesc: Your data will now be decrypted in your browser and saved as a JSON file.
export.securityDesc: The export file contains your data in **plaintext**. Store this file in a secure location, preferably on an encrypted external drive.
export.faqFormat: What format is my data in?
export.faqFormatAnswer: The export is a decrypted JSON file in plaintext. It can be opened with any text editor or imported into other password managers.
export.faqAttachments: Are attachments included in the export?
export.faqAttachmentsAnswer: This manual JSON export does not include attachments. However, attachments are included in the emergency export (ZIP), which is created automatically during an emergency release.
export.faqDifference: What is the difference to the emergency export?
export.faqDifferenceAnswer: This export is for personal use and requires the master password. The emergency export (ZIP) is for emergency contacts – it is fully encrypted and can only be decrypted with the release key.

vault.importTooltip: Import from 1Password, Bitwarden, Chrome, Firefox, Apple, KeePass, LastPass, Dashlane or generic CSV
vault.templateTooltip: Vorsorge inventory: predefined templates for bank account, insurance, subscription, ...

contacts.title: Emergency Contacts
contacts.name: Name
contacts.newContactDesc: Add a trusted person for emergencies.
contacts.fillRequired: Please fill in name and email
contacts.discretionDesc: Your emergency contacts will only be notified when the emergency plan is triggered.
contacts.shamirNote: In Shamir mode, each contact receives only a fragment — no one can decrypt alone.
contacts.notAllSupplied: Not all contacts supplied. Please regenerate.
contacts.distributeDesc: Generate and distribute the key fragments to your contacts.
contacts.emailModeDesc: Each contact receives an individual code by email. The fragments are encrypted client-side with this code — Heritavo never sees the code. In an emergency, the contact enters their code in viewer.html.
contacts.printModeDesc: A code is displayed per contact, which you print and hand over physically. The fragments are stored encrypted. In an emergency, the contact enters their code in viewer.html.
contacts.zkWarning: Zero-Knowledge: If a code or fragment is lost, you can redo the setup — your entries remain intact. If a code or fragment is compromised, a full key rotation is recommended. Treat codes and fragments like a physical key.
contacts.codesGenerated: {count} codes generated and sent by email.
contacts.allSuppliedMessage: All {count} contacts have been supplied. Setup complete.
contacts.printWarningDesc: Print this page or note the values securely before continuing. Heritavo cannot recover them.
contacts.testamentDesc: Your contacts will receive a download link by email in an emergency. You saved the recovery key during setup. If you lost it, you can reveal it again under Settings.

settings.title: Settings
settings.checkinIntervalDesc: How often do you want to check in?
settings.gracePeriodDesc: How long should we wait after the interval?
settings.releaseModeDesc: How should your data be released in an emergency?
settings.shamirEmailWarning: In email mode, access codes are sent by email to your emergency contacts. This mode is therefore not fully zero-knowledge — the codes pass through the mail server. For maximum security choose "Shamir — Printed code" and hand the codes over in person.
settings.deleteAccountDesc: All data will be permanently deleted.
settings.deleteAccountConfirm: Really delete your account? This action cannot be undone.
settings.languageDesc: Choose the language for the interface and emails.
settings.releaseModeDescription: Determine how your vault is released in an emergency. Changing modes requires re-completing the Shamir setup.
settings.shamirEmailSub: Convenient, but not fully zero-knowledge (server sends codes)
settings.shamirPrintSub: Hand over code physically, decryption in viewer
settings.securityNoteDesc: In email mode, access codes are sent by email to your emergency contacts. This mode is therefore not fully zero-knowledge — the codes pass through the mail server. For maximum security choose "Shamir — Printed code" and hand the codes over in person.
settings.setupReminder: After saving, the setup must be re-completed under Emergency Contacts before the vault can be filled.
settings.emailNotVerifiedDesc: Please confirm your email address to use all features.
settings.recoverySheetDesc: Download your recovery sheet again and store it in a secure location (safe, notary, bank vault).
settings.shamirActiveDesc: Your vault will be released via your emergency contacts in an emergency – no recovery sheet needed.
settings.shamirConfigInfo: Configuration: {threshold} of {total} contacts must agree. Setup and distribution of codes or fragments is done under Emergency Contacts.
settings.shamirZkNote: Zero-Knowledge: If a code or fragment is lost, you can simply redo the setup — your entries remain intact. If a fragment is compromised, a full key rotation is recommended (new setup, re-save all entries).
settings.deleteAccountDescription: Permanently deletes your account, all vault entries, contacts, releases, and files.
settings.deleteIrreversible: This action is irreversible. All your data will be deleted immediately and completely. Enter your password to confirm.
settings.keyNotFound: Key data not found. Please log in again.
settings.serverError: Server error loading key data. Please try again.
settings.modeChange1: The Shamir setup must be re-completed (redistribute codes/fragments).
settings.modeChange2: Existing vault entries remain fully intact and do not need to be re-saved.
settings.modeChange3: Until the setup is completed, no new entries can be added.
settings.modeChangeConfirmLabel: I understand that I must re-complete the setup with my contacts.

onboarding.title: Replay onboarding
onboarding.desc: Show the setup wizard again — useful to check your progress or discover new features.

settings.profileSectionDesc: Personal settings for the greeting in your emails.
settings.displayNameHint: Appears as the greeting in our emails ('Hello Anna,'). Leave empty if you'd rather just have 'Hello,'.
settings.newsletterSectionDesc: Control whether you'd like to receive occasional updates by email. Transactional emails (e.g. 2FA, emergency release) are unaffected.
settings.newsletterStatusActive: Active – you receive Heritavo updates by email.
settings.newsletterStatusPending: Confirmation pending – check your inbox for our confirmation email.
settings.newsletterStatusInactive: Inactive – we don't send you any newsletter.
settings.newsletterEnabledMessage: Confirmation email sent. Click the link inside to activate sending.
settings.newsletterDisabledMessage: You've been unsubscribed from the newsletter.

billing.switchNoChange: No price change for the current period

household.transferConfirmText: Transfer ownership to {email}? You will become a regular member.
household.transferBillingWarning: The existing subscription will be cancelled. The new owner must set up their own payment method.

release.title: Heritavo Emergency Release
release.downloaded: This export has already been downloaded.
release.enterCodesDesc: At least {threshold} of {total} codes are required:
release.notEnoughCodes: Not enough valid codes — {count} of {threshold} required
release.noEntries: No entries found in vault (API returns 0 entries).
release.noReleaseKey: {count} entry/entries have no release key. Please unlock the vault in the dashboard and reload, then try again.
release.wrongKey: {count} entry/entries could not be decrypted — reconstructed key does not match. Please check your codes.

shamir.loadError: Error loading. Please reload the page.
shamir.alreadySubmittedDesc: You have already submitted your fragment successfully. Thank you.
shamir.vaultReleasedDesc: Enough codes have been submitted. The encrypted export is ready. You will also receive an email with this link.
shamir.fragmentSubmittedDesc: Your fragment was submitted successfully ({submitted} of {threshold} required). The other emergency contacts must also submit their fragments.
shamir.shamirInstructions: An emergency release has been triggered. Please enter your personal emergency code to submit your fragment.
shamir.codeReceivedHint: You received this code by email when you were added as an emergency contact.
shamir.codeInvalid: Code invalid or fragment could not be decrypted. Please check your code.
shamir.codeLocalOnly: Your code is only used locally in your browser and is never sent to the server in plain text.

unsubscribe.unsubscribedDesc: You will no longer receive emails as an emergency contact.
unsubscribe.resubscribedDesc: You will receive emails as an emergency contact again.
unsubscribe.activeDesc: You receive emails when used as an emergency contact.
unsubscribe.inactiveDesc: You do not receive emails as an emergency contact.
unsubscribe.disclaimer: This page only affects notifications as an emergency contact. Your Heritavo account (if any) is not affected.

verification.subject: Heritavo – Please verify your email address
verification.title: Verify email address
verification.lead: Before you can set up your digital vault, we need to confirm this email belongs to you. Just click the button below — that's it.
verification.expiry: The link expires in 24 hours. If it expires, you can request a new one anytime from the dashboard.
verification.ignore: You didn't sign up? Just ignore this message — nothing happens unless you click.

twoFactor.subject: Heritavo – Your sign-in code
twoFactor.title: Your login code
twoFactor.lead: Enter this code in your browser window to complete the sign-in.
twoFactor.warning: If you didn't expect this email, someone may have tried to sign in with your address. Your password alone is NOT enough to log in — without this code, the attempt fails. If you suspect misuse, change your password.

passwordReset.subject: Heritavo – Password reset
passwordReset.title: Reset your password
passwordReset.lead: You — or someone who knows your email — requested a password reset for Heritavo. Use the button below to set a new password.
passwordReset.expiry: The link is valid for 1 hour.
passwordReset.warning: IMPORTANT: If you forget your master password, Heritavo cannot recover your encrypted vault data — we don't know your password. Resetting deletes all current vault content and you start with an empty vault.
passwordReset.ignore: If you didn't request a reset, ignore this message — your existing password stays active.

checkinReminder.subject: Heritavo – Time for your check-in
checkinReminder.title: Your check-in is due
checkinReminder.lead: Your next Heritavo check-in is coming up. A quick login confirms you're reachable and prevents the automatic emergency release of your vault.
checkinReminder.whatItMeansBody: Heritavo requires regular check-ins (login + confirm) so your emergency contacts are NOT notified unnecessarily. If you don't sign in on time, the release triggers automatically.
checkinReminder.support: If you're going to be unreachable for a while (travel, hospital), you can extend the check-in interval anytime.

releaseWarning.subject: Heritavo – Final warning before emergency release
releaseWarning.title: Final warning
releaseWarning.lead: Your Heritavo check-in is overdue. If you don't sign in within the next {warningHours} hours, your vault's emergency release will trigger automatically and your contacts will be notified.
releaseWarning.whatToDoBody: Just sign in to confirm your check-in. The clock resets and nothing else happens.
releaseWarning.support: If you're truly unreachable and the release is intended, do nothing. Questions? support@heritavo.com

releaseTriggered.subject: Heritavo – Your vault's emergency release was triggered
releaseTriggered.title: Emergency release triggered
releaseTriggered.lead: Your Heritavo vault's automatic emergency release has been triggered because you didn't confirm the last check-in within the configured window.
releaseTriggered.whatItMeansBody: Your emergency contacts now receive a link to the encrypted vault archive. They can only decrypt it with your release code (from the recovery sheet) or their Shamir fragments — the server alone cannot open it.
releaseTriggered.whatToDoBody: You can still revoke the release within the grace period via your dashboard, if you're reachable. The vault itself stays encrypted — your master password is still required to decrypt anything.

contactRelease.subject: Heritavo – Important: you've been activated as an emergency contact
contactRelease.title: You have access to a digital legacy
contactRelease.lead: {ownerEmail} listed you as an emergency contact for their Heritavo vault. The release was just activated — either because {ownerEmail} didn't confirm the regular check-in, or because a trustee manually triggered the emergency flow.
contactRelease.whatItMeansBody: Use the link below to download the encrypted vault archive. To decrypt, you need the release code (from the recovery sheet you received) OR your Shamir code share if Shamir mode was active.
contactRelease.whatToDoBody: Heritavo itself cannot decrypt the contents — the keys live only with you and the other emergency contacts.
contactRelease.expiryNote: The download link is valid until {availableUntil}. If it expires, contact support@heritavo.com — we can help.
contactRelease.support: Not sure what to do? Email support@heritavo.com

shamirCode.subject: Heritavo – Your Shamir code share
shamirCode.title: Your code share
shamirCode.lead: {ownerEmail} listed you as an emergency contact for their Heritavo vault and chose Shamir's Secret Sharing as the emergency mode. Here is your share of the vault key.
shamirCode.whatItMeansBody: Store this code in a safe place (password manager, secure notes app). In an emergency — when the release triggers automatically or manually — you contacts together will decrypt the vault content.
shamirCode.whatToDoHeading: How it works in an emergency
shamirCode.whatToDoBody: You'll receive an email with a download link. In the viewer tool, enter your code share — once enough shares come together, the data can be decrypted.
shamirCode.support: Heritavo itself does NOT know this code — we cannot resend it. If you lose it, ask {ownerEmail} to issue a new one.

freePlanExpiry.subject: Heritavo – Your free plan expires soon
freePlanExpiry.title: Free plan ends in {daysLeft} days
freePlanExpiry.lead: Your Heritavo free plan ends in {daysLeft} days. To prevent your vault data from being deleted, you can upgrade to a paid plan now or deliberately deactivate your account.
freePlanExpiry.whatItMeansHeading: What happens if you do nothing
freePlanExpiry.whatItMeansBody: After expiry, your data is permanently deleted after a 30-day grace period. Heritavo cannot restore it afterwards.
freePlanExpiry.whatToDoBody: You can upgrade to a paid plan (more vault slots, video legacies, multi-user), stay on the free plan (with limits), or export and delete your account.

dormantAccount.subject: Heritavo – Your inactive account will be deleted soon
dormantAccount.title: Account will be deleted in {daysUntilDeletion} days
dormantAccount.lead: You haven't signed in to Heritavo for {daysUntilDeletion} days. Since your vault's emergency release has already been executed, your account will be automatically deleted soon — unless you take action.
dormantAccount.whatItMeansBody: After a successful emergency release we keep the account for 6 more months in case you want to come back. If you don't, the account, vault and all data will be permanently removed.
dormantAccount.whatToDoHeading: If you want to keep the account
dormantAccount.whatToDoBody: Just log in. That resets the inactivity timer and you can also set up a new vault.

planLimitExceeded.subject: Heritavo – Plan limit reached
planLimitExceeded.title: You have limit overages
planLimitExceeded.lead: Your current plan exceeds the allowed limits in one or more areas. Heritavo doesn't delete data — but new entries or actions in the affected areas are blocked.
planLimitExceeded.whatItMeansBody: Details on the affected areas are in your dashboard. You can either delete entries, clean up specific areas, or upgrade to a higher plan.
planLimitExceeded.whatToDoBody: Review the details and decide whether to switch to a more suitable plan or consolidate existing entries.

quotaReminder14.subject: Heritavo – Plan limit exceeded for 14 days
quotaReminder14.title: Plan limit still exceeded
quotaReminder14.lead: You have been over your plan limit for 14 days. New entries remain blocked. Your existing data is safe and unchanged.
quotaReminder14.whatItMeansHeading: What happens if you take no action
quotaReminder14.whatItMeansBody: Per our Terms of Service, we reserve the right to restrict permanently over-quota accounts after 90 days. Until then, everything stays as it is today.
quotaReminder14.whatToDoBody: Either consolidate/delete entries until you are back under the limit, or upgrade to a more suitable plan.

quotaReminder30.subject: Heritavo – Plan limit exceeded for 30 days
quotaReminder30.title: Final notice before account restriction
quotaReminder30.lead: You have been over your plan limit for 30 days. Please act within the next few weeks — either consolidate entries or upgrade your plan.
quotaReminder30.whatItMeansBody: After 90 days over limit, your account will be set to read-only (viewing + emergency release continue to work, new entries are permanently blocked). This measure is governed by our Terms of Service.
quotaReminder30.whatToDoBody: Open your dashboard, review per-area usage, and decide by day 60 what you would like to do.
quotaReminder30.support: Feel free to reach out: support@heritavo.com

releaseCancelledOwner.subject: Heritavo – Emergency release revoked
releaseCancelledOwner.title: Emergency release was revoked
releaseCancelledOwner.lead: The triggered emergency release of your Heritavo vault was revoked — either by you, by a veto-click from the trigger email, or because you signed in again in time.
releaseCancelledOwner.whatItMeansBody: The vault stays protected. Your emergency contacts are NOT (any longer) notified, the release link is deactivated, and any ZIP archive that was created has been deleted.
releaseCancelledOwner.whatToDoBody: Sign in briefly to confirm your check-in — that resets the clock. If you'd like to change the check-in interval, you can do so in the dashboard.

releaseCancelledContact.subject: Heritavo – Emergency release no longer active
releaseCancelledContact.title: The emergency release was revoked
releaseCancelledContact.lead: The emergency release for which you were notified as a contact is no longer active. The vault owner returned in time or revoked the release.
releaseCancelledContact.whatItMeansBody: The download link from the previous email no longer works. The vault stays protected and only the original person retains access.
releaseCancelledContact.whatToDoBody: You don't have to do anything. If a release is triggered again, you'll get a fresh email with a valid link.

releaseReady.subject: Heritavo – Vault archive ready for download
releaseReady.title: Your vault archive is ready
releaseReady.lead: As announced, the encrypted vault archive is now available for your emergency contacts. This message confirms everything went as planned.
releaseReady.whatItMeansBody: The emergency contacts receive a separate download link. You can also view the archive via the dashboard — for example to check that everything you wanted is included.

invite.subject: Heritavo – You've been invited to a household
invite.title: Invitation to a Heritavo household
invite.intro: {inviter} invited you to the household {household}. You can create your own vault that runs on the family/couple subscription — your own data, your own contacts, but shared billing.
invite.about: Heritavo is a digital legacy vault: you store encrypted what should reach your trusted persons in an emergency (passwords, documents, video legacies). When the time comes, your emergency contacts are activated automatically or manually.
invite.step1: 1. Accept the invitation via the button below
invite.step2: 2. Set a password — only YOU know it; Heritavo cannot recover it
invite.step3: 3. Set up your vault and emergency contacts
invite.expiry: The invitation is valid for {ttlDays} days.
invite.security: Security note: this link is personal — don't forward it.
invite.ignore: Not expecting an invite from {inviter}? Just ignore this email.

registrationAttempt.subject: Heritavo – A registration attempt with your email was detected
registrationAttempt.title: Duplicate registration detected
registrationAttempt.lead: Someone tried to register a new Heritavo account with your email address. Since you already have an account, the registration was prevented.
registrationAttempt.whatItMeansBody: If it was you (e.g. forgot your password): use the login page and reset via 'Forgot password?'. If it wasn't you: ignore this message — your account is safe and no one accessed it.

releaseExtended.subject: Heritavo – Download link extended
releaseExtended.title: Availability extended
releaseExtended.intro: One of your emergency contacts extended the download link for your vault archive by {extensionDays} days. This is normal behavior — emergency contacts can do this multiple times if they need longer to secure the data.
releaseExtended.explanation: If you suspect someone is extending without authorization: contact support@heritavo.com — we can deactivate the link immediately.

trusteeInvited.subject: Heritavo – {ownerEmail} would like to add you as a trusted person
trusteeInvited.title: You've been listed as a trusted person
trusteeInvited.lead: {ownerEmail} listed you as a trusted person on Heritavo. This means: in an emergency — when {ownerEmail} is no longer reachable — you can manually trigger the vault's emergency release.
trusteeInvited.whatItMeansBody: You should be able to judge whether a real emergency exists (death, serious illness, accident). If yes, you press the emergency trigger in your own Heritavo account. There's a grace period during which {ownerEmail} can veto via link — for example if they're actually still reachable.
trusteeInvited.whatToDoBody: If you want to take this on: create a free Heritavo account and accept the invitation. You don't need your own vault — the trustee role is independent.
trusteeInvited.expiry: The invitation link is valid for {ttlDays} days.
trusteeInvited.ignore: You don't know {ownerEmail}? Just ignore this email.

manualTriggerWarning.subject: Heritavo – {trusteeEmail} triggered the emergency flow
manualTriggerWarning.title: Emergency trigger activated
manualTriggerWarning.lead: Your trusted person {trusteeEmail} just manually triggered the emergency release flow for your Heritavo vault. The release is revocable by you during the grace period.
manualTriggerWarning.alertBody: If you do not veto, your emergency contacts will be notified on {expiryHuman} and will receive access to the encrypted vault.
manualTriggerWarning.whatItMeansBody: Trusted persons trigger this when they believe you're in a real emergency (death, serious illness, prolonged unreachability). That's intended — but if the assumption is wrong, you can revoke.
manualTriggerWarning.whatToDoHeading: If this happened by mistake or unauthorized
manualTriggerWarning.whatToDoBody: Click the revoke button. The trigger stops immediately, your emergency contacts are not informed, and the vault stays protected.
manualTriggerWarning.okayNote: If this trigger is legitimate (e.g. you're truly unreachable): do nothing — the release runs through automatically.

manualTriggerVetoed.subject: Heritavo – {ownerEmail} revoked your emergency trigger
manualTriggerVetoed.title: Emergency trigger revoked
manualTriggerVetoed.lead: {ownerEmail} revoked the emergency trigger you activated on {triggeredHuman}. The vault stays protected and the emergency contacts will NOT be notified.
manualTriggerVetoed.whatItMeansBody: {ownerEmail} returned in time or used the veto link from the warning email. This is the normal 'false alarm' situation — no one did anything wrong.
manualTriggerVetoed.whatToDoBody: You can trigger again later if it turns out the situation is actually serious. Until then, do nothing.

trusteeAccountDeleted.subject: Heritavo – Your trusted person {trusteeEmail} deleted their account
trusteeAccountDeleted.title: Trusted person no longer active
trusteeAccountDeleted.lead: {trusteeEmail} was listed as your trusted person and just deleted their Heritavo account. The trustee status was automatically revoked — this person can no longer trigger an emergency release for your vault.
trusteeAccountDeleted.whatItMeansBody: If {trusteeEmail} was your only trusted person, you no longer have manual emergency escalation (you're not entirely without protection — automatic release via check-in continues).
trusteeAccountDeleted.whatToDoBody: Add a new trusted person — ideally someone who knows you well and would respond reliably in an emergency.

manualTriggerCancelled.subject: Heritavo – Emergency trigger automatically revoked
manualTriggerCancelled.title: Trigger automatically ended
manualTriggerCancelled.lead: The emergency trigger that {trusteeEmail} activated on {triggeredHuman} was automatically revoked — because the trusted person has since deleted their Heritavo account.
manualTriggerCancelled.whatItMeansBody: Your vault will NOT be released. The emergency contacts will NOT be notified. The previous veto link is now obsolete.
manualTriggerCancelled.whatToDoBody: If you still want a trusted person, you can add a new one. If this trigger was 'accidental' from the deleted person — all good, the vault stays safe.

trusteeInviteExpired.subject: Heritavo – Trusted person invitation to {invitedEmail} expired
trusteeInviteExpired.title: Invitation expired
trusteeInviteExpired.lead: Your invitation to {invitedEmail} as a trusted person was not accepted and has now expired. {invitedEmail} has no trustee status — manual emergency triggers via this person are no longer possible.
trusteeInviteExpired.whatToDoBody: You can resend the invitation (e.g. if the first one ended up in spam) or choose another person as trusted person. Both go via the dashboard.

ownershipTransfer.subject: Heritavo – You transferred household ownership
ownershipTransfer.title: Ownership transferred
ownershipTransfer.lead: You transferred ownership of household {householdName} to {newOwnerEmail}. Subscription, billing, and member management now go entirely to them.
ownershipTransfer.whatItMeansBody: You remain a household member and keep your own vault and emergency contacts. What changes: you can no longer invite others or change the plan — that's now {newOwnerEmail}'s job. Your existing Stripe subscription was canceled; {newOwnerEmail} needs to set up a new one.
ownershipTransfer.support: If the transfer was an accident, contact us within 7 days: support@heritavo.com

newOwnerBilling.subject: Heritavo – You're the new household owner
newOwnerBilling.title: You're taking over ownership
newOwnerBilling.lead: You are now the owner of household {householdName}. To keep the plan active, you need to set up a new Stripe subscription — the previous one was canceled with the transfer.
newOwnerBilling.alertHeading: Action needed: add a payment method
newOwnerBilling.alertBody: Until you set up the new subscription, the household runs in 'past_due' status — write actions for other members may be blocked. Please add a payment method soon.
newOwnerBilling.whatItMeansBody: You now manage: plan changes, member invitations, member removals. Vaults and emergency contacts of individual members remain off-limits to you — they stay zero-knowledge encrypted and accessible only to the respective members.

paymentFailed.subject: Heritavo – Payment failed for {tenantName}
paymentFailed.title: Payment failed
paymentFailed.lead: A payment for your Heritavo household {tenantName} failed — usually because the card expired, there's no balance, or the bank declined the charge.
paymentFailed.whatItMeansHeading: What happens if you do nothing
paymentFailed.whatItMeansBody: We'll automatically retry over the next few days. If payment continues to fail, the subscription is canceled after about 14 days total and the household falls back to the FREE plan (with limit warnings if you're over FREE limits). Your vault data and emergency contacts remain — even without payment — they will NOT be deleted.
paymentFailed.whatToDoBody: Update the payment method in the dashboard. As soon as the next charge succeeds, everything runs normally again.

onboardingReminder.subject: Heritavo – Finish your setup
onboardingReminder.title: Your Heritavo setup is almost complete
onboardingReminder.lead: Your Heritavo account is ready — but the emergency setup isn't complete yet. For the vault to truly protect what it should, a few short steps remain.
onboardingReminder.whatItMeansBody: Without emergency contacts no one receives the vault content in an emergency — it stays encrypted forever. Without vault entries the vault is empty and there's nothing to pass on. Both steps are needed for Heritavo to fulfill its purpose.
onboardingReminder.whatToDoBody: Sign in and finish the setup — typically about 10 minutes. You can start small (1 emergency contact + 1 vault entry) and expand over time.

email.footer: This message was sent by Heritavo.
email.copyright: © Heritavo · Moritz Hauff IT · Tägerwilen, Switzerland
email.unsubscribeNote: You receive this email because your Heritavo account is configured accordingly.

newsletterConfirm.subject: Heritavo – Please confirm your newsletter subscription
newsletterConfirm.title: Confirm newsletter subscription
newsletterConfirm.lead: You signed up for occasional Heritavo updates and tips by email. To start sending we just need one confirmation click — standard procedure (double opt-in).
newsletterConfirm.expiry: The confirmation link is valid for 7 days.
newsletterConfirm.ignore: If you didn't sign up for our newsletter, just ignore this email — we won't send anything.

unverifiedAccount.subject: Heritavo – Please confirm your email
unverifiedAccount.title: Account will be removed in {daysUntilDeletion} days
unverifiedAccount.lead: You created a Heritavo account a while back but haven't confirmed your email address yet. To keep your account active and unlock your vault, we just need a quick confirmation.
unverifiedAccount.whatItMeansBody: If we don't receive a confirmation in the next {daysUntilDeletion} days, your account will be automatically removed. There's no sensitive data in your vault yet — you can sign up again any time.
unverifiedAccount.whatToDoBody: Click the button below — we'll send a fresh confirmation link to this address. One click in the email and you're set.

setupIncomplete.subject: Heritavo – your vault setup is incomplete
setupIncomplete.title: No emergency contacts set
setupIncomplete.lead: You currently have no emergency contacts set. In an emergency, nobody can access your vault – the automatic release would have no recipient. Please add at least one trusted person so your digital legacy reaches the right people.
setupIncomplete.whatItMeansBody: Heritavo only releases your encrypted data to the emergency contacts you configure. Without contacts, no one can receive your vault in an emergency. You can change this anytime in the Emergency contacts section.
setupIncomplete.support: You can add, change, or remove contacts later – what matters is that at least one person is set.

viewer.title: Heritavo Offline Decryption
viewer.subtitle: Everything happens locally in your browser · No internet required
viewer.enterCodes: Enter the personal codes of the emergency contacts

errors.rateLimited: Too many requests. Please wait a moment.
errors.emailSendFailed: Email could not be sent. Please try again.

register.title: Create account
register.subtitle: Set up your household and start with peace of mind.
register.subtitleSimple: Email and password are all you need — set up the vault after login.
register.masterPasswordHint: Your master password is never sent to us. If you lose it, your data is irrecoverably lost.
register.step2Title: How should your vault be released?
register.step2Subtitle: Choose how your trusted contacts get access in an emergency. You can change this later.
register.zkWarningText: Heritavo cannot recover your code or fragment. If lost, you can regenerate codes in the Dashboard. If stolen, we recommend a complete regeneration.
register.shamirDashboardHint: In Shamir mode, you must complete the setup under Emergency Contacts after login before the vault can be used.
register.shamirStep3Codes: Generate and distribute codes to contacts — then fill the vault
register.shamirStep3Fragments: Generate and hand over fragments to contacts — then fill the vault
register.vaultLockedTitle: Vault locked until setup is complete
register.vaultLockedText: No vault entries can be created until the Shamir setup is complete.
register.shamirConfirmCheckbox: I understand that the vault can only be used after completing the setup under Emergency Contacts.
register.recoveryKeySubtitle: Save this key now in a safe place. It will never be shown again.
register.lastChanceText: This key is the only way to decrypt your vault in an emergency. Heritavo cannot recover it. Print it and store it safely.
register.recoveryConfirmCheckbox: I have securely stored my Recovery Key and understand that Heritavo cannot recover it.
register.modeTestamentDesc: You receive a Recovery Key to print and store in a safe or with a notary.
register.modeShamirEmailDesc: Each contact receives a personal code by email during setup. In an emergency, they enter the code in the viewer.
register.modeShamirPrintDesc: You print a code for each contact and hand it over in person.
register.displayNameHint: Used as greeting in emails ('Hello Anna,'). Leave empty if you'd rather not.
register.agbCheckboxLabel: I have read and accept the <agb>Terms</agb> and <privacy>Privacy Policy</privacy>.
register.agbCheckboxRequired: You must accept the Terms and Privacy Policy to continue.
register.newsletterCheckboxLabel: I'd like to receive occasional Heritavo updates and tips by email (unsubscribe anytime).
register.newsletterConfirmSent: We've sent you a confirmation email. Sending starts only after you click the link.

errors.fillRequired: Please fill in name and email.

vaultSetup.title: Set up your vault now?
vaultSetup.subtitle: You can also use Heritavo without your own vault (e.g. as a trustee only). If you want to create your own vault, do it now — or skip this step and set it up later.
vaultSetup.passwordHint: We derive the key from it — it never reaches our servers.
vaultSetup.modeLabel: How should trusted contacts unlock the vault in an emergency?

testament.label: Recovery key (testament)
testament.desc: You receive a key to print and store securely.

shamir_code_email.label: Shamir codes by email
shamir_code_email.desc: Each emergency contact receives a code by email.

shamir_code_print.label: Shamir codes to print
shamir_code_print.desc: You print one code per contact and hand it over in person.

vaultSetup.skipHint: You can skip this step and set up the vault later from the dashboard.
vaultSetup.successSubtitle: You can now save entries and add trusted contacts.
vaultSetup.recoveryWarning: Save this recovery key NOW in a safe place. It will not be shown again.
vaultSetup.passwordWrong: The password doesn't match your master password. Please enter the same password you use to sign in — otherwise the vault cannot be decrypted later.

welcome.title: Welcome to Heritavo
welcome.subtitle: Let's get your account ready in 3 minutes — so your family knows what they need to know.
welcome.duration: You can pause anytime and continue later.

steps.one: Designate a trusted person for emergencies

contacts.title: Who should know in case of emergency?
contacts.subtitle: At least one person who will get access to your vault when it matters.
contacts.savedSubtitle: You can add more people later.

recovery.testamentSubtitle: In Testament mode, the key lives in your Recovery Sheet — keep it safe.
recovery.shamirTitle: Distribute codes to your trusted people
recovery.shamirSubtitle: In Shamir mode, each emergency contact needs their personal code.

printed.testament: Recovery Sheet printed or saved as PDF

stored.testament: Stored in a safe place (safe, notary, bank deposit box)
stored.shamir: Codes distributed to all emergency contacts

tested.testament: You know where the sheet is — even 5 years from now
tested.shamir: Contacts know how to use the code in an emergency

distributed.testament: Recovery Sheet printed or saved as PDF

instructed.testament: Stored in a safe place (safe, notary, bank deposit box)
instructed.shamir: Codes distributed to all emergency contacts

firstEntry.title: Add your first entry
firstEntry.subtitle: Something your family will really need in an emergency.

suggestions.important: Important contracts or insurance policies (as PDF attachment)

firstEntry.skippable: You can skip this step and come back later.

done.title: Heritavo is ready!
done.subtitle: You've laid the foundation. Expand your setup whenever you want.

reminder.title: Setup {pct}% complete
reminder.subtitle: Click here to see the next steps.

email.subject: Heritavo: Your setup isn't finished yet
email.title: Forgot to finish your setup?
email.intro: You created your Heritavo account a few days ago — but your setup isn't complete yet. For your family to actually get access in an emergency, a few steps are still missing.
email.ignoreText: If you no longer want to use Heritavo, you can delete your account from the settings.

passwordImport.header: Guide — How do I export my passwords?
passwordImport.subheader: Pick your password manager. Heritavo supports 8 common formats.
passwordImport.noneSelected: Pick your password manager to see the steps.

1password.title: 1Password
1password.subtitle: CSV export from the desktop app or web vault.
steps: Set format to CSV, choose location.
Confirm with master password — the CSV is saved.
1password.warning: The exported CSV is unencrypted — upload to Heritavo immediately, then securely delete.

bitwarden.title: Bitwarden
bitwarden.subtitle: JSON (recommended) or CSV from the web vault.
steps: Choose format: JSON (complete, with folders) or CSV.
bitwarden.warning: Export file is unencrypted — upload to Heritavo immediately, then securely delete.

apple.title: Apple Passwords
apple.subtitle: macOS 15+ / iOS 17+ — via System Settings.
steps: macOS: System Settings → Passwords (or the new Passwords app). iOS: Settings → Passwords.
Confirm with Touch ID / Face ID / device passcode.
apple.warning: Only works on macOS 15 / iOS 17 or newer. Older versions need iCloud → Keychain Export (more complex).

chrome.title: Google Chrome
chrome.subtitle: From the built-in password manager.
steps: Confirm with your computer password (system login).

lastpass.title: LastPass
lastpass.subtitle: Note: LastPass emails the CSV to you.
steps: Open the web vault (lastpass.com), sign in.
LastPass emails the CSV — check inbox, attach.
lastpass.warning: The CSV comes via email. Delete the mail after Heritavo import — it contains all passwords in plain text.

dashlane.title: Dashlane
dashlane.subtitle: CSV export from the web account.
steps: Open the web app (app.dashlane.com), sign in.
Confirm with master password, save file.
dashlane.warning: Dashlane mobile app cannot export CSV — use the web version.

generic.title: Other / generic CSV manager
generic.subtitle: Any CSV file with manual column mapping.
steps: Export your passwords as CSV from your manager.
Heritavo will show you the columns found — map them (Name, Username, Password, …).

vaultTemplates.subheader: Predefined entry templates so your family has the overview in an emergency.
vaultTemplates.footerHint: You can freely adjust the fields after choosing.

newsletterResult.confirmedBody: Thanks! You'll now receive occasional Heritavo updates. You can unsubscribe anytime via the link in any email or in your settings.
newsletterResult.expiredBody: The confirmation link has expired or already been used. You can restart the subscription in your account settings.
newsletterResult.invalidBody: This link is invalid. Please check that you've copied the full link from the email.

trusteeAccept.title: Become a trustee
trusteeAccept.ownerInviteIntro: wants to add you as a trustee.
trusteeAccept.bullet1: In an emergency (illness, accident, death) you can manually trigger the vault release.
trusteeAccept.bullet2: {owner} gets a 7-day grace period to revoke.
trusteeAccept.bullet3: If there's no response → the emergency contacts are notified and get access to the encrypted vault.
trusteeAccept.noVaultNeeded: You don't need your own vault — being a trustee is enough.
trusteeAccept.wrongAccountBody: You're logged in as {currentEmail}. The invitation is for {inviteEmail}.
trusteeAccept.switchAccount: Sign out and log in with the correct email
trusteeAccept.successBody: You are now a trustee for {owner}. The trigger button appears in your dashboard under "Trustees".

inviteAccept.title: Heritavo invitation
inviteAccept.description: You've been invited to the household {household}. Create your account at {email}. You'll set up the vault after login.
inviteAccept.passwordHint: Never sent to us. Lose it = lose your data.

quota.overQuotaDesc: You are over the limit in at least one area of your plan. New entries, attachments and invitations are blocked until you clean up or upgrade. Existing data is preserved.
quota.pastDueDesc: Your last subscription payment is overdue. Please update your payment method in the Stripe portal to resume creating new entries.
